page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the ARM64 architecture, this server features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz using the AWS Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 design. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it provides a high-speed network interface with a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks highlight the efficiency of the Neoverse-V2 architecture. The instance achieves top-tier results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core execution, and software compilation using Clang. Memory subsystem performance is highly competitive, with both read and write large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks ranking in the top 10% tier. Additionally, the platform demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests and excels in static web serving throughput and database operations.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the c8gb.12xlarge is driven by its high physical core density and ARM-based architecture, which delivers strong compute and memory bandwidth performance per resource unit compared to traditional x86 alternatives. However, workloads requiring local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration will face tradeoffs, as these components are absent. This instance is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, multi-threaded data decompression, high-throughput web serving, and database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by the 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU hardware, focusing resources entirely on compute density. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-core processing, software compilation, memory bandwidth, and database operations. This hardware profile makes the c8gb.12xlarge highly efficient for compute-bound tasks, multi-threaded decompression, high-throughput web serving, and distributed database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.12xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 server with 48 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 96 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.12xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 server featuring 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 96 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps networking.
tagline: A dedicated 48-core ARM64 compute-optimized platform delivering high-bandwidth memory performance and 100 Gbps networking for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARM64 physical cores utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 architecture
96.0 GB of system memory delivering top-tier read and write bandwidth
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfers
Top-tier benchmark performance in software compilation and database operations
Optimized for multi-threaded decompression, web serving, and compute-bound workloads
